title: quercus faginea subvar. pedunculata
intro: Quercus faginea subvar. pedunculata is a subvariety of the evergreen Quercus faginea tree, also known as the Portuguese oak or French oak, which is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Quercus faginea subvar. pedunculata include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as exhibiting potential antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of this plant may be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health and overall well-being.
